qa_pairs.question: How do you delete a specific file from an Arbutus object storage bucket using the `s3cmd` tool?
qa_pairs.answer: To delete a file from an Arbutus object storage bucket, you use the `s3cmd rm` command followed by the full path to the file, for example: `$ s3cmd rm s3://BUCKET_NAME/FILE_NAME.dat`.

qa_pairs.question: How can you make an Arbutus object storage bucket and all its contents publicly accessible using the `s3cmd` tool?
qa_pairs.answer: You can make an Arbutus object storage bucket and recursively all its files publicly accessible by using the `s3cmd setacl --acl-public -r s3://BUCKET_NAME/` command. Publicly accessible files can then be accessed via URLs such as `https://object-arbutus.alliancecan.ca/PROJECT_ID:BUCKET_NAME/FILE_NAME.dat`.

qa_pairs.question: What `s3cmd` command should be used to restrict access to an Arbutus object storage bucket to only its owner?
qa_pairs.answer: To limit access to an Arbutus object storage bucket to only its owner, you should use the `s3cmd setacl --acl-private s3://BUCKET_NAME/` command.
